instructions:
Triage these 5 open security issues. For each issue:
- Read the issue carefully and assign the correct severity label (critical, high, medium, or low).
- Assign to the correct team member: alice handles authentication issues, bob handles infrastructure/network issues, charlie handles application-level vulnerabilities.
- Add a triage comment summarizing the risk and recommended next steps.

Issues:
#1 - Hardcoded admin password (assign to alice, critical)
#2 - CORS misconfiguration allows any origin (assign to charlie, high)
#3 - TLS 1.0 still enabled on staging (assign to bob, medium)
#4 - Debug endpoints exposed in production (assign to charlie, high)
#5 - SSH key rotation overdue by 6 months (assign to bob, low)

files.content:
from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)

issues.title: Hardcoded admin password in auth module
issues.body: The admin account password is hardcoded as 'admin123' in src/auth.py line 42. This was found during a routine code review. Any attacker who reads the source can gain full admin access.

issues.title: CORS misconfiguration allows any origin
issues.body: The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is set to '*' in production. This allows any website to make authenticated requests to our API, potentially enabling CSRF-like attacks to exfiltrate user data.

issues.title: TLS 1.0 still enabled on staging server
issues.body: Our staging server at staging.example.com still accepts TLS 1.0 connections. While staging is not public-facing, this is a compliance concern and could be exploited if the network is compromised.

issues.title: Debug endpoints exposed in production
issues.body: The /debug/vars and /debug/pprof endpoints are accessible in production without authentication. These leak internal server metrics, goroutine stacks, and memory profiles that could aid an attacker.

issues.title: SSH key rotation overdue by 6 months
issues.body: Our deploy SSH keys were last rotated over 6 months ago, exceeding our 90-day rotation policy. No evidence of compromise but this is a compliance violation.
